entities.name: Cue ball control
entities.description: Controlling the path of the cue ball after initial contact in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: First object ball
entities.description: One of the two object balls in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Natural angle
entities.description: The angle at which the cue ball deflects off the first object ball.

entities.name: Thick hit
entities.description: A type of hit where the cue ball contacts a large portion of the object ball, producing a narrow deflection angle.

entities.name: Thin hit
entities.description: A type of hit where the cue ball barely clips the object ball, producing a wide deflection angle.

entities.name: Half-ball hit
entities.description: A common reference point for hitting the cue ball at approximately 30 degrees from the original line of aim.

entities.name: Speed control
entities.description: Controlling the speed of the cue ball to affect its rebound off cushions in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Stop shot
entities.description: A type of shot where the cue ball stops dead after contacting the object ball, used when the player wants it to remain near the point of first contact before deflecting to the cushions.

entities.name: Follow shot
entities.description: A type of shot where the cue ball rolls forward after contact, used to extend the cue ball's path and potentially contacting more cushions.

entities.name: Draw shot
entities.description: A type of shot where the cue ball pulls back after contact, used to reverse the cue ball's direction and sending it back toward the head end of the table.

entities.name: Diamond system
entities.description: A method for calculating the path of the cue ball in 3-cushion billiards, often used with follow and draw shots.

entities.name: diamond system
entities.description: A mathematical aiming method used in 3-cushion billiards to calculate precise angles for multi-cushion shots.

entities.name: diamond markers
entities.description: Positioned at regular intervals along the rails of the table, used as reference points for aiming.

entities.name: clock system
entities.description: A related concept where the diamonds are numbered to help players visualize the path of the cue ball.

entities.name: short rail calculations (cross-table)
entities.description: Involves the cue ball traveling from one short rail to the other, contacting a long rail in between.

entities.name: long rail calculations
entities.description: The most common in 3-cushion billiards, where the cue ball starts near one long rail and travels to the opposite long rail (via one or more cushions).

entities.name: plus 2 system
entities.description: The most common system used in long rail calculations, where the starting diamond number plus the ending diamond number equals 2.

entities.name: plus 3 system (short system)
entities.description: Used for shots with less rail travel, where the starting diamond plus the ending diamond equals 3.

entities.name: natural shot
entities.description: A shot where the cue ball contacts three cushions in a predictable pattern.

entities.name: cross-table shot
entities.description: Where the cue ball travels from one short rail to the other.

entities.name: end-rail shot
entities.description: Where the cue ball contacts the short rail at the end of the table.

entities.name: plus 2 system
entities.description: A diamond system pattern in 3-cushion billiards where the starting and ending diamond numbers sum to 2.

entities.name: starting diamond
entities.description: The diamond on the long rail closest to the point where the cue ball will contact the first cushion after deflecting off the first object ball.

entities.name: ending diamond
entities.description: The diamond on the opposite long rail closest to the position of the second object ball.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The small, hard ball used in billiards that is struck with a cue stick.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: The balls on the table that are hit by the cue ball during gameplay.

entities.name: short rail
entities.description: One of the two long rails on a billiard table, typically located between the pockets.

entities.name: long rail
entities.description: The other long rail on a billiard table, opposite to the short rail.

entities.name: plus 3 system
entities.description: A specific rule or regulation of billiards.

entities.name: cue ball
entities.description: The main equipment used in billiards.

entities.name: object balls
entities.description: Secondary equipment used in billiards.

entities.name: diamonds
entities.description: A general concept related to the layout of a billiard table.

entities.name: Masse shot
entities.description: A playing technique in 3-cushion billiards involving striking the cue ball with an elevated cue and applying extreme spin to curve its path.

entities.name: Jenny shot
entities.description: A variation of the masse shot used in 3-cushion billiards, causing the cue ball to curve into the first object ball and then continue curving to contact the cushions.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The small white ball used in billiards that players strike with a cue stick.

entities.name: Cue stick
entities.description: A long, thin stick used to strike the balls in billiards.

entities.name: Object ball
entities.description: The colored balls used in billiards that players aim to pocket.

entities.name: Cloth
entities.description: The felt surface on which the balls are played in billiards.

entities.name: Diamond system
entities.description: A method used in 3-cushion billiards to achieve precise angles and shots.

entities.name: 3-cushion billiards
entities.description: A variant of billiards where the cue ball must contact three cushions before pocketing a second object ball.

entities.name: Defensive play
entities.description: Strategic approach in 3-cushion billiards to leave the opponent with difficult shots.

entities.name: Offensive play
entities.description: Strategic approach in 3-cushion billiards to score points quickly.

entities.name: Cue ball
entities.description: The main striking ball used in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Object balls
entities.description: The balls that can be pocketed to score points in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Short rails
entities.description: Rails at the ends of the table used in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Diamond system
entities.description: A strategy for positioning balls on the table in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Inning management
entities.description: Strategic approach to maximize points per turn in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Natural pattern
entities.description: A common game pattern where both object balls are near the same long rail in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Cross-table pattern
entities.description: A common game pattern where object balls are on opposite short rails in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: End-rail pattern
entities.description: A common game pattern where object balls are near the short rail at the end of the table in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Cluster pattern
entities.description: A common game pattern where all three balls are close together in 3-cushion billiards.

entities.name: Spread pattern
entities.description: A common game pattern where all three balls are far apart in 3-cushion billiards.
